generators.js_source: async(e,t)=>{let a=new Intl.NumberFormat(void 0,{notation:"compact",compactDisplay:"short",maximumSignificantDigits:3}),i=e[e.length-1];if(i.includes("@")&&!i.startsWith("@")){let[n,o]=i.split("@"),s=encodeURIComponent(n),{stdout:l}=await t({command:"curl",args:["-sfL",`https://crates.io/api/v1/crates/${s}/versions`]});return JSON.parse(l).versions.map(p=>({name:`${n}@${p.num}`,insertValue:`${p.num}`,description:`${a.format(p.downloads)} downloads - ${new Date(p.created_at).toLocaleDateString()}`,hidden:p.yanked}))}else if(i.length>0){let n=encodeURIComponent(i),[{stdout:o},{stdout:s}]=await Promise.all([t({command:"curl",args:["-sfL",`https://crates.io/api/v1/crates?q=${n}&per_page=60`]}),t({command:"cargo",args:["metadata","--format-version","1","--no-deps"]})]),r=JSON.parse(o).crates.sort((c,g)=>g.recent_downloads-c.recent_downloads).map(c=>({icon:"\u{1F4E6}",displayName:`${c.name}@${c.newest_version}`,name:c.name,description:`${a.format(c.recent_downloads)}${c.description?` - ${c.description}`:""}`})),p=[];return s.trim().length>0&&(p=JSON.parse(s).packages.filter(g=>!g.source).map(g=>({icon:"\u{1F4E6}",displayName:`${g.name}@${g.version}`,name:g.name,description:`Local Crate ${g.version}${g.description?` - ${g.description}`:""}`}))),r.concat(p)}else return[]}

generators.js_source: async(e,t)=>{let a=new Intl.NumberFormat(void 0,{notation:"compact",compactDisplay:"short",maximumSignificantDigits:3}),i=e[e.length-1];if(i.includes("@")&&!i.startsWith("@")){let[n,o]=i.split("@"),s=encodeURIComponent(n),{stdout:l}=await t({command:"curl",args:["-sfL",`https://crates.io/api/v1/crates/${s}/versions`]});return JSON.parse(l).versions.map(p=>({name:`${n}@${p.num}`,insertValue:`${p.num}`,description:`${a.format(p.downloads)} downloads - ${new Date(p.created_at).toLocaleDateString()}`,hidden:p.yanked}))}else if(i.length>0){let n=encodeURIComponent(i),[{stdout:o},{stdout:s}]=await Promise.all([t({command:"curl",args:["-sfL",`https://crates.io/api/v1/crates?q=${n}&per_page=60`]}),t({command:"cargo",args:["metadata","--format-version","1","--no-deps"]})]),r=JSON.parse(o).crates.sort((c,g)=>g.recent_downloads-c.recent_downloads).map(c=>({icon:"\u{1F4E6}",displayName:`${c.name}@${c.newest_version}`,name:c.name,description:`${a.format(c.recent_downloads)}${c.description?` - ${c.description}`:""}`})),p=[];return s.trim().length>0&&(p=JSON.parse(s).packages.filter(g=>!g.source).map(g=>({icon:"\u{1F4E6}",displayName:`${g.name}@${g.version}`,name:g.name,description:`Local Crate ${g.version}${g.description?` - ${g.description}`:""}`}))),r.concat(p)}else return[]}

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Execute all unit and integration tests and build examples of a local package

name: --test
options.description: Test only the specified test target

name: --bench
options.description: Test only the specified bench target

name: -j
--jobs
options.description: Number of parallel jobs, defaults to # of CPUs

name: --profile
options.description: Build artifacts with the specified profile

name: --features
options.description: Space or comma separated list of features to activate

name: -Z
options.description: Unstable (nightly-only) flags to Cargo, see 'cargo -Z help' for details

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Display one character per test instead of one line

name: --lib
options.description: Test only this package's library unit tests

name: --no-fail-fast
options.description: Run all tests regardless of failure

name: --workspace
options.description: Test all packages in the workspace

name: -r
--release
options.description: Build artifacts in release mode, with optimizations

name: --future-incompat-report
options.description: Outputs a future incompatibility report at the end of the build

name: -v
--verbose
options.description: Use verbose output (-vv very verbose/build.rs output)

name: --frozen
options.description: Require Cargo.lock and cache are up to date

name: --locked
options.description: Require Cargo.lock is up to date

subcommands.name: tree
subcommands.description: Display a tree visualization of a dependency graph

name: -p
--package
options.description: Package to be used as the root of the tree

name: --features
options.description: Space or comma separated list of features to activate

name: --target
options.description: Filter dependencies matching the given target-triple (default host platform). Pass `all` to include all targets

name: -e
--edges
options.description: The kinds of dependencies to display (features, normal, build, dev, all, no-normal, no-build, no-dev, no-proc-macro)

name: -i
--invert
options.description: Invert the tree direction and focus on the given package

name: --prune
options.description: Prune the given package from the display of the dependency tree

name: --depth
options.description: Maximum display depth of the dependency tree

name: --prefix
options.description: Change the prefix (indentation) of how each entry is displayed

name: --charset
options.description: Character set to use in output: utf8, ascii

name: -f
--format
options.description: Format string used for printing dependencies

name: -Z
options.description: Unstable (nightly-only) flags to Cargo, see 'cargo -Z help' for details

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Do not print cargo log messages

name: --workspace
options.description: Display the tree for all packages in the workspace

name: --no-dedupe
options.description: Do not de-duplicate (repeats all shared dependencies)

name: -d
--duplicates
options.description: Show only dependencies which come in multiple versions (implies -i)

name: -v
--verbose
options.description: Use verbose output (-vv very verbose/build.rs output)

name: --frozen
options.description: Require Cargo.lock and cache are up to date

name: --locked
options.description: Require Cargo.lock is up to date
